what_it_does: Defines the VOICE face as structured guidance for verbal design: invariant voice tokens, punctuation rules, registers, verbal forms, lexicon constraints, sequence rules, and lintable invariants.
observations: Voice tokens require first-person-plural agency, present active tense, candid confidence, gerund mission phrasing, no hedging, telegraphic compression, and number-led claims
Casing rules distinguish titles, super headings, bullets, labels, and coined terms
Registers are named immersive, tension, explanatory, credible, and precise, each with word guidance and evidence references
Forms bind verbal archetypes to layout slots, molecule names, chrome controls, or sequence roles
Several forms include length budgets, examples, evidence strings, and optional molecule bindings
The lexicon separates preferred verbal material from banned hedge/filler/superlative language
Invariants identify predicate-checkable and model-judged voice rules
standouts: Verbal forms are explicitly paired with visual slots or molecule names
The file treats figures as claim-bearing sentence units rather than supporting decoration
The checker model is part of the authored contract through predicate/model invariant labels
conventions: Uses descriptive JSON objects grouped by voice area
Uses _note fields for local explanation inside sections
Uses compact shape/is wording for form definitions
Uses evidence strings that cite deck pages, slide ranges, or corpus harvest references
Uses id, applies_to, predicate, message, and checker for invariants
Mixes scalar examples and example arrays depending on form
concerns: Some binds values are prose strings rather than structured references
Evidence references are human-readable strings, not normalized objects
The hedge invariant says hedge subset but points to lexicon.dont_words, which also includes filler and superlative terms
notes: This file is a verbal design ledger tying voice behavior to layout contexts, examples, evidence, and lint rules.
questions: Which exact subset of lexicon.dont_words counts as hedging for voice-no-hedging?
Are binds strings intended to be machine-resolved or only read by humans?
Should evidence references become structured citations with source, page, and excerpt fields?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it describes the verbal medium of the design DNA and provides forms, registers, rules, and invariants to govern it.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to make brand voice operational by turning verbal style into named forms, register controls, examples, and checker-ready constraints.
novelty: It links copywriting forms directly to visual molecules and layout slots while also encoding voice rules as predicate/model invariants.
contribution: Contributes a structured verbal substrate for a fused system to keep: voice tokens, register taxonomy, form bindings, lexicon exclusions, evidence-backed examples, and checker-oriented invariants.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON voice specification defining verbal tokens, casing, registers, copy forms, lexicon rules, and invariants for the counterpart-design VOICE face.
intention_for_embedding: This was for operationalizing a design voice so generated or reviewed text can align with named registers, visual bindings, and lintable constraints.
